text.title: prompt-basics Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for prompt-basics as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Zero-shot fails on novel output schemas — model guesses format without examples
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Model has no reference for the new schema in the training data; zero-shot works only for standard schemas.

failure-mode.description: Few-shot example quality matters more than quantity; bad examples poison output
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Model learns from examples; bad examples teach the wrong pattern more effectively than good ones.

failure-mode.description: Prompt injection via user-controlled content can override system prompt constraints
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Mixing instruction text and user data in the same string is indistinguishable to the model without boundaries.

failure-mode.description: Long, nested system prompts degrade following accuracy on complex tasks (model "forgets" later constraints)
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Model attention to instructions drops with distance from the current query.

failure-mode.description: Without versioning, prompt drift goes undetected — silent regression in output quality
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Runtime-constructed prompts leave no audit trail; changes are invisible to code review.

failure-mode.description: Chain-of-Thought adds latency and tokens; not appropriate for high-throughput pipelines
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: CoT adds 100–500 tokens per call; overhead not justified for simple tasks.

failure-mode.description: Asking the model to "return ONLY JSON" still fails occasionally — always try/except json.loads and retry with a corrective message
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Model understanding of "only" is not perfect; defensive parsing is required.

failure-mode.description: Temperature 0 is not fully deterministic across providers or after model updates — do not assume identical outputs in production
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Determinism varies by provider and model version; testing required.
